mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and supports multiple expansion, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are followed by weak equity performance or if higher rates do not precede lower forward returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher after declining FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in a rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, which should reduce equity returns over the next month. The mechanism is confirmed when rising unemployment is followed by negative S&P 500 forward returns and fails when unemployment shocks do not coincide with drawdowns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the rolling 3-year correlation is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in retail sales, captured by RSAFS, indicates resilient consumer demand and supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if strong sales growth is not associated with positive equity drift.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS levels or increases are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the rank correlation stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while UNRATE is falling, real activity is improving faster than labor slack, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is specific to synchronized industrial and labor improvement and fails if either leg does not reinforce the other.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE does not outperform each series alone, or if triggered instances do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a housing slowdown that feeds into weaker consumer demand and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect should be visible only when housing starts break below a recent baseline, and it fails if housing weakness is offset by other growth measures.
failure_condition: Reject if sub-10% housing starts do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign is positive in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A high and stable CPIAUCSL combined with FEDFUNDS above the inflation trend signals real policy tightness, which compresses valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is strongest when inflation is still elevated and policy rates are restrictive, and it fails if inflation falls faster than rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation above its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-tight-policy condition does not lead to negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional rank IC is not negative in a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE with continued PAYEMS growth indicates a still-tight labor market and sustained household income, which supports risk assets and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is short-horizon and fails when payroll growth stalls even if unemployment stays low.
failure_condition: Reject if low unemployment plus payroll growth does not improve 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the sign reverses during two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand deterioration that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is regime-dependent and is expected to work only when both measures are decelerating together, not when GDP weakens alone.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail sales deceleration fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is no better than either proxy alone over any 3-year rolling window.
